pr.title: docs(architecture): document multi-tenant SaaS divergence from SF
pr.body:
## Summary

- Adds a new section (~310 words) to `docs/SYSTEM_DESIGN.md` explaining why this clone deliberately diverges from Screaming Frog's single-user desktop architecture.
- Covers storage (Postgres+Redis vs embedded Derby/`.seospider`), frontend (React SPA vs Swing/JavaFX), API surface (Axum + JWT + `tenant_id`), scheduling (job system vs `--headless`), and deployment (EC2/SSM/ECR vs JVM installer).
- Calls out license/activation parity and exact `.seospider` byte layout as deliberately out-of-scope.

Implements slice C.3 of the SF-clone gap-closure master design.

## Test plan

- [ ] Render `docs/SYSTEM_DESIGN.md` in a Markdown previewer and confirm the new section 10 sits cleanly between section 9 (Open Questions) and section 11 (Change log).
- [ ] Skim the new section to confirm it stays in the 200-400 word target and reads as orientation for a new contributor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
pr.author: sahiljani
